etymology.description: semantic 貝(貝) (bèi, "cowry") as 'value' + phonetic 否(否) (fǒu) -> bear burdens -> bear; suffer
etymology.interpretation: In 負(負) (fù, "bear; suffer"), the 貝(貝) (bèi, "cowry") represents carrying value or burdens, while 否(否) (fǒu) supplies the sound. The idea of carrying a burden connects to 'bear' and the related weight of responsibility links to 'suffer'.
etymology.simplification: The traditional 負(負) was simplified by removing the 貝(貝) (bèi, "cowry") radical, retaining only the main strokes to reduce its complexity, reflecting 否(否) (fǒu).

examples.chinese: 他负担了家庭的所有开支(他負擔了家庭的所有開支)。
examples.pinyin: Tā fùdānle jiātíng de suǒyǒu kāizhī.
examples.english: He bore all the household expenses.

examples.chinese: 这个项目失败后(這個項目失敗後)，他感到非常失望(他感到非常失望)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège xiàngmù shībài hòu，tā gǎndào fēicháng shīwàng.
examples.english: After the project failed, he felt very disappointed.

examples.chinese: 她不堪负荷(她不堪負荷)，决定放弃这份工作(決定放棄這份工作)。
examples.pinyin: Tā bùkān fùhè，juédìng fàngqì zhè fèn gōngzuò.
examples.english: She couldn't bear the load and decided to quit the job.
